text: Excuse me, could you tell me how to {{c1::get to}} the nearest train station?
explanation: get to is the most common conversational phrasal verb meaning to reach or arrive at a destination.
usage:
Pattern: get to [place]
  • How do I get to the museum from here?
  • It takes 20 minutes to get to the office.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿podría decirme cómo llegar a la estación de tren más cercana?

text: Go straight ahead for two blocks, then {{c1::take a left}} at the traffic light.
explanation: To 'take a left' is a very common idiomatic phrase meaning to turn left at a specific intersection or landmark.
usage:
Pattern: take a left / make a left / turn left
  • Take a left at the next street.
  • You will need to take a left after you pass the school.
spanish: Siga derecho por dos cuadras, luego doble a la izquierda en el semáforo.

text: Excuse me, is there a pharmacy {{c1::within walking distance}} of here?
explanation: 'Within walking distance' means close enough to be reached easily by walking (typically less than 10-15 minutes away).
usage:
Pattern: within walking distance [of something]
  • The hotel is within walking distance of the beach.
  • We live within walking distance of the office.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿hay alguna farmacia a corta distancia a pie de aquí?

text: You can't miss it; it's right {{c1::across from}} the post office.
explanation: 'Across from' means directly opposite or on the other side of a street, hallway, or space.
usage:
Pattern: across from [place] / opposite
  • The cafe is across from the library.
  • He sat across from me during the meeting.
spanish: No tiene pérdida; está justo enfrente de la oficina de correos.

text: To get to the museum, you need to {{c1::cross the street}} and walk past the bank.
explanation: To 'cross the street' means to walk from one side of a road to the opposite side.
usage:
Pattern: cross the street / cross the road
  • Look both ways before you cross the street.
  • We crossed the street to avoid the construction work.
spanish: Para llegar al museo, debe cruzar la calle y pasar el banco.

text: Excuse me, could you {{c1::point me in the direction of}} the nearest subway station?
explanation: To 'point someone in the direction of' means to show or tell someone the general path or heading to reach a destination.
usage:
Pattern: point [someone] in the direction of [place]
  • Can you point me in the direction of the restrooms?
  • She pointed us in the direction of the highway entrance.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿podría indicarme la dirección hacia la estación de metro más cercana?

text: The stadium is located on the {{c1::outer edge}} of the city.
explanation: The 'outer edge' refers to the boundary, periphery, or outskirts of an area, such as a city limit.
usage:
Pattern: outer edge / outskirts
  • They built a new retail park on the outer edge of town.
  • We live on the outer edge of the metropolitan area.
spanish: El estadio está ubicado en las afueras/extremo exterior de la ciudad.

text: If you hit the gas station, you've {{c1::gone too far}}; turn back.
explanation: To 'go too far' means to travel past the intended destination or target point.
usage:
Pattern: go too far / pass the destination
  • If you reach the river, you've gone too far.
  • I think we went too far; we should turn around.
spanish: Si llega a la gasolinera, se ha pasado; regrese.

text: Take the second exit at the {{c1::roundabout}} to stay on Main Street.
explanation: A 'roundabout' (or traffic circle) is a circular intersection where drivers travel counterclockwise (or clockwise in UK) around a central island.
usage:
Pattern: roundabout / traffic circle
  • Go straight through the first roundabout.
  • Turn right at the roundabout.
spanish: Tome la segunda salida en la rotonda para permanecer en la calle principal.

text: Is it quicker to walk or to {{c1::hail a cab}} from here?
explanation: To 'hail a cab' (or hail a taxi) means to signal a taxi on the street (typically by raising your arm) to stop and pick you up.
usage:
Pattern: hail a cab / catch a taxi
  • We stood in the rain trying to hail a cab for fifteen minutes.
  • It is easy to hail a cab in this neighborhood.
spanish: ¿Es más rápido caminar o pedir/hacerle señas a un taxi desde aquí?
